faq.headline: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about QR codes and QRcodly.
faq.answer1: A QR code (Quick Response Code) is a smart, two-dimensional barcode that can store various types of information, such as website links, text, or contact details. It can be easily captured and deciphered using a smartphone or a dedicated scanner.
faq.question2: How do I create a QR code?
faq.answer2: With qrcodly.de, you can create your custom QR code in no time: simply enter the desired content and download your freshly generated code directly. Quick and straightforward!
faq.question3: What is the difference between static and dynamic QR codes?
faq.answer3: Static QR codes contain fixed information that cannot be changed after creation. Dynamic QR codes, on the other hand, offer maximum flexibility: you can adjust the linked content at any time after creation and also benefit from advanced features like detailed scan statistics.
faq.answer4: Yes, the generation of both static and dynamic QR codes is and will remain free forever, as many as you want. In the future, there will be limitations for API requests and for businesses working with multiple users.
faq.question5: How long is a QR code valid?
faq.answer5: Static QR codes do not expire and remain permanently valid as long as the linked content is accessible online. Dynamic QR codes remain active as long as they are managed in our system and no specific restrictions apply from the provider.
faq.question6: What are the benefits of dynamic QR codes?
faq.answer6: Dynamic QR codes are marked with the Dynamic badge. They can be updated at any time, even after being created or printed. They also provide useful scan statistics – such as location, time, and devices used – so you can analyze and optimize your campaigns effectively.
faq.question7: Do I need a special app to scan QR codes?
faq.answer7: No, in most cases, it's not necessary! The integrated camera function of modern smartphones can now directly recognize QR codes and open the embedded content – no additional app required.
faq.question8: Which statistics are collected for dynamic QR codes?
faq.answer8: When you enable statistics, each scan of your dynamic QR code records and analyzes information such as the number of scans, visitors, devices used, countries, browsers, and operating systems.
faq.question9: Can I personalize a QR code with my logo?
faq.answer9: Yes, with qrcodly.de, you can design your QR codes exactly to your liking: integrate your logo and use your brand colors without affecting scanability. This way, your codes remain unique and professional!
faq.question10: Do QR codes also work in print?
faq.answer10: Absolutely! QR codes are specifically optimized for printing and work reliably on various materials, as long as they remain clear and undamaged. Ensure good print quality, and your codes will be ready for use anywhere!
faq.answer11: All data at QRcodly is stored exclusively on servers located in Germany. We use Clerk for authentication and privacy-friendly analytics to improve our service. Your data is not sold or shared for advertising purposes.

general.a1: A QR code (Quick Response Code) is a smart, two-dimensional barcode that can store various types of information, such as website links, text, or contact details. It can be easily captured and deciphered using a smartphone or a dedicated scanner.
general.q2: How do I create a QR code?
general.a2: With QRcodly, you can create your custom QR code in no time: simply enter the desired content and download your freshly generated code directly. Quick and straightforward!
general.q3: Do I need a special app to scan QR codes?
general.a3: No, in most cases, it's not necessary! The integrated camera function of modern smartphones can now directly recognize QR codes and open the embedded content — no additional app required.
general.q4: Do QR codes also work in print?
general.a4: Absolutely! QR codes are specifically optimized for printing and work reliably on various materials, as long as they remain clear and undamaged. Ensure good print quality, and your codes will be ready for use anywhere!
general.a5: All data at QRcodly is stored exclusively on servers located in Germany. We use Clerk for authentication and privacy-friendly analytics to improve our service. Your data is not sold or shared for advertising purposes.
general.a6: Yes! QRcodly is fully open source and available on GitHub. You can inspect the code, contribute, or even self-host your own instance.
general.q7: Can I use QRcodly for commercial purposes?
general.a7: Absolutely. QRcodly is free for both personal and commercial use. Create QR codes for your business, marketing campaigns, product packaging, or any other commercial application.

qrCodes.q1: Is the QR code generator free?

qrCodes.a5: Yes. Every dynamic QR code tracks scans in real time, showing total scans, unique visitors, device types, browsers, operating systems, and geographic locations.

qrCodes.q7: Can I create QR codes in bulk?
qrCodes.a7: Yes! You can create QR codes in bulk using CSV import. Upload a CSV file with your content, and QRcodly generates all codes at once. You can also use templates for consistent styling across bulk-created codes.
qrCodes.q8: What is a QR code template?
qrCodes.a8: A QR code template saves your preferred design settings — colors, logo, corner style, and more — so you can instantly apply them to new QR codes without reconfiguring each time.
qrCodes.q9: What image formats can I download QR codes in?
qrCodes.a9: You can download your QR codes as PNG, SVG, or WEBP files. SVG is ideal for print materials as it scales without losing quality.

urlShortener.a1: Yes! QRcodly's URL shortener is free to use. You can create short URLs, track clicks, and manage your links at no cost. Pro plans unlock advanced features like custom domains.

urlShortener.q6: What's the difference between short URLs and QR codes?
urlShortener.a6: Short URLs are clickable links you share digitally (email, social media, ads). QR codes are scannable images for physical media (print, packaging, signage). Both track analytics and can use custom domains.
urlShortener.q7: What happens if I delete a short URL?
urlShortener.a7: When you delete a short URL, it becomes permanently inactive. Anyone clicking the link will see a 404 page. This action cannot be undone, so consider disabling the URL instead if you may need it later.
urlShortener.q8: Can I create short URLs via the API?
urlShortener.a8: Yes. QRcodly provides a REST API that lets you create, update, and manage short URLs programmatically. Check out the API documentation for details and authentication setup.

pricing.q2: How do I upgrade to Pro?
pricing.a2: You can upgrade to Pro directly from your dashboard. Go to the Plans page, choose your preferred billing cycle, and complete the checkout. Your Pro features activate instantly.
pricing.q3: Can I cancel my subscription anytime?
pricing.a3: Yes. You can cancel your Pro subscription at any time from your dashboard. You'll continue to have access to Pro features until the end of your current billing period.
pricing.q4: Do I need an account to create QR codes?
pricing.a4: No! You can create and download static QR codes without an account. However, signing up (free) unlocks dynamic QR codes, analytics, URL shortening, and dashboard management.
